title: Stenostomum abbreviatum
taxonomy: Stenostomum abbreviatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Stenostomum and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This classification places Stenostomum abbreviatum within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Stenostomum abbreviatum is further classified as a member of the subfamily Gentianoideae and the tribe Gentianeae. The exact classification of Stenostomum abbreviatum may vary depending on the source and taxonomic revisions.

intro: Stenostomum abbreviatum, also known as Short-stemmed Stenostomum or Short-stemmed Gentian, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Gentianaceae family. Traditionally, Stenostomum abbreviatum has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents like gentiopicroside, gentiogenin, and swertiamarin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Stenostomum abbreviatum is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
